summary: Bundle regen + verification strategy is structurally sound. Bundled-JS grep catches what source grep alone misses (post-esbuild minification/renaming), and the 4 residuals (event log --data, context write --data, packet create --file/--content, help text) are all legitimate whitelist entries preserving JSON input, context blob write, and file-copy semantics. TTY sim with NO_STDIN stderr and non-zero exit proves the hang-guard boundary. Windows shebang wrap note is a real cross-platform finding.
concerns: Windows shebang: spawnSync cannot invoke plugin/bin/geas directly via shebang; requires process.execPath wrapper. Downstream tooling (CI, dashboards) invoking the bundle as a script on Windows will need the same workaround — worth a platform note in rules.md.
macOS/Linux shebang path was not exercised; low risk but CI should cover before release.
Whitelist is currently implicit (grep + human judgment). Consider a committed allowlist file or regression test so future --data/--file additions force explicit review.
